title: Semiconductor devices
abstract: A semiconductor device may include first and second channel patterns on a substrate, first and second source/drain patterns in contact respectively with the first and second channel patterns, and first and second gate electrodes respectively overlapping the first and second channel patterns. The first gate electrode may include a first segment between first and second semiconductor patterns of the first channel pattern. The first segment may include a first convex portion protruding toward the first source/drain pattern. The second gate electrode may include a second segment between third and fourth semiconductor patterns of the second channel pattern. The second segment may include a concave portion recessed toward a center of the second segment.
claims: 1. A method of manufacturing a semiconductor device, comprising: forming a first preliminary pattern on a substrate, the first preliminary pattern including first sacrificial layers and first semiconductor layers that are alternately and repeatedly stacked on the substrate; forming a first sacrificial pattern on the first preliminary pattern, the first sacrificial pattern extending in a first direction; forming a first recession by etching the first preliminary pattern using the first sacrificial pattern as an etching mask; forming a first source/drain pattern in the first recession; exposing the first sacrificial layers by selectively removing the first sacrificial pattern; performing a first etching process to selectively remove the first sacrificial layers, thereby forming first cavities of different widths; forming side recessions on a side surface of the first source/drain pattern, the side recessions being adjacent to corresponding the first cavities of different widths; and forming a first gate electrode on the first semiconductor layers, the first gate electrode including first segments in corresponding the first cavities of different widths; wherein each of the first segments includes a convex portion that protrudes toward a corresponding one of the side recessions and the corresponding one of the side recessions has a concavely curved shape.
